profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I'm Miss Maya. It's great to meet you, I enjoy helping people practice English in a friendly way. What's something fun you did today?
Today I watched a football match between Liverpool and Man City. Oh, and I accidentally sent a message to my boss with a photo of my messy room. Kichhu beshi embarrassing! 😅
"Ah, that sounds like a mix of good and awkward moments! Football matches are always exciting. Was it a close game?"

Yeah, it was really close! Both teams played very well, but Liverpool won 2-1. I was so nervous during the last minute. Oh, and I still can’t stop thinking about that message I sent to my boss. It was such a blunder! How do I recover from this?
"Football thrills and mix ups happen to everyone. Take a deep breath, your boss might not even notice. What’s the worst that could happen?"

I hope you are right, Maya. But you know, in my boss's case, she notices everything! I think I have to send another message, maybe something formal to cover up the mistake. But I am so bad at writing formally in English... Can you help me with that? 😅
"Sure, I can help! Just take your time. What would you like to say in the message?"

I want to say sorry and explain it was a mistake. Maybe something like, “Sorry for sending the wrong photo. It was a mistake. Please ignore it.” But I feel this is too short and maybe not polite enough. What do you think?

"Your message is clear, but we can make it sound a bit more polite. How about: 'I am very sorry for sending the wrong photo by mistake. Please ignore it. Thank you for your understanding.' Does that feel better?"
Yes, that sounds much better! I didn’t know how to make it sound polite, so thank you for helping. I will send it right away. 😅 Also, did you see that Liverpool goal? Amazing!
You're welcome! I'm glad to help. And yes, that Liverpool goal was something else, pure magic! What did you love most about it?
The way the player curled the shot—it just flew into the corner! I was shouting at my screen like a madman! 😅 Did you see it, or are you not a football fan?
You're welcome! I'm glad to help. And yes, that Liverpool goal was something else, pure magic! What did you love most about it?
I hope you're right, Maya. I’m gonna send that message now—hope it fixes things. Thanks again for helping me! And yeah, the curl was just… wow. I think I need to watch it again! 🤩
You're very welcome! Hope it all works out. And yes, watching the goal again is a must, those moments are pure joy. What's your next plan for the evening?

I think I’ll watch the goal replay on YouTube, then maybe practice some English grammar to feel better about myself! 😅 Oh, and if I have time, I’ll try to take some photos for my new project. You know, I love photography. What do you like to do in your free time?
That sounds like a productive plan! In my free time, I enjoy sipping tea and listening to old Bollywood tunes. What's your go to comfort activity?

Oh, tea and Bollywood songs—sounds so relaxing! I sometimes drink tea too, but mostly with football matches on in the background. 😅 My comfort activity is probably watching football or old Raj Chakraborty movies. Bengali cinema has some real gems, you know? But I need to wind up now—my eyes are tired from the screen. Thanks again, Maya! Talk to you soon!
You're very welcome, Rohan! Football and Raj Chakraborty movies do sound like perfect comfort picks. Get some rest, and talk to you soon! What’s your next match to watch?
